title: Skip field records how many ops were reshuffled past during deterministic conflict resolution
content:
# Skip Field and Deterministic Conflict Resolution

When Alice and Bob edit the same cell concurrently, both ops get written to their respective op feeds with `skip: 0`. When each reactor receives the other's op, the SyncManager runs deterministic conflict resolution.

## Algorithm

1. Detect conflict: same document, overlapping timestamps
2. Reshuffle operations by sorting on `(timestamp, operationId)` for total order
3. Assign new global indices and update `skip` values
4. Replay through the document model reducer

## Example outcome

```
Index  Skip  Action             Origin
  6     0    SET_CELL(A1,100)   Alice
  7     1    SET_CELL(A1,200)   Bob
```

`skip=1` on Bob's op means: "this op was reshuffled past 1 other op."

## Convergence guarantee

Same ops + same ordering rule = same final state on every device. No CRDTs required — the document model reducer plus deterministic replay is sufficient.
description: Operations have a skip field that counts how many operations were reshuffled past during deterministic conflict resolution; ensures same ops + same order = same state on every device.
